title: Skills Registry Integration
description: Add sl skill subcommand integrating with Vercel skills.sh registry for discovering, installing, and auditing reusable AI agent skills. Native Go client, no Node.js dependency. Ref: GitHub issue #94.

title: Setup: Package structure and VCR dependency
description: WHY: All implementation tasks need the pkg/cli/skills/ package and go-vcr v4 test dependency in place before work can begin.
design: Create pkg/cli/skills/ package directory. Add gopkg.in/dnaeon/go-vcr.v4 to go.mod (test dependency). Create tests/testdata/cassettes/skills/ for VCR cassettes. Reference: /Users/vids/hkjc/tfc-cli/internal/tfc/client_test.go for VCR setup pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Package compiles. go-vcr v4 available in test imports. Cassette directory exists.

title: Foundational: Source parser, HTTP client, lock file, hashing
description: WHY: All 6 sl skill subcommands depend on these shared building blocks — source parsing, API client, lock file I/O, and folder hashing.

items.item: source.go parses owner/repo@skill and full git URLs
client.go queries search, audit, and GitHub APIs
hash.go computes deterministic SHA-256 folder hash

design: 4 modules in pkg/cli/skills/: source.go (ParseSource using cligit.ParseRepoFlag/ParseRepoURL), client.go (Client struct with configurable base URLs), lock.go (Vercel-compatible skills-lock.json), hash.go (SHA-256 folder hash). Each module has _test.go with VCR cassettes or table-driven tests.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 modules compile, have passing tests, and can be imported by command implementations.

title: Implement source identifier parser
description: WHY: All sl skill commands that accept a source argument need to parse owner/repo@skill shorthand and full git URLs into a structured SkillSource.
design: File: pkg/cli/skills/source.go. Implement ParseSource(input string) (*SkillSource, error). Split on @ to extract SkillFilter. For owner/repo shorthand, delegate to cligit.ParseRepoFlag() (pkg/cli/git/git.go:77-84), set Type=github. For full URLs (https://, git@, ssh://), delegate to cligit.ParseRepoURL() (git.go:54-65), set Type=git. SkillSource struct: Owner, Repo, SkillFilter, Ref (default main), Type (github|git), URL. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/source-parser.ts for format examples.
acceptance_criteria: Parses owner/repo, owner/repo@skill, https://github.com/owner/repo, git@github.com:owner/repo.git. Returns structured SkillSource. Errors on invalid input with actionable message.

title: Implement skills.sh HTTP client
description: WHY: Search, audit, info, and telemetry commands all need an HTTP client to call skills.sh and GitHub APIs.

items.item: Base URLs configurable via env vars
VCR cassette tests for all 3 endpoints

design: File: pkg/cli/skills/client.go. Client struct with SearchURL, AuditURL, GitHubURL fields (configurable via SKILLS_API_URL, SKILLS_AUDIT_URL, GITHUB_API_URL env vars for testability). Methods: Search(query string, limit int) ([]SkillSearchResult, error) calls GET skills.sh/api/search?q=&limit=. FetchAudit(source string, slugs []string) (map[string]SkillAuditResult, error) calls GET add-skill.vercel.sh/audit?source=&skills=. FetchSkillContent(owner, repo, ref, path string) ([]byte, error) fetches from raw.githubusercontent.com. NewClient() reads env vars with defaults. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/find.ts:32-59 for search, /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/add.ts:108-154 for audit. VCR cassettes: record responses to tests/testdata/cassettes/skills/. Reference VCR pattern: /Users/vids/hkjc/tfc-cli/internal/tfc/client_test.go.
acceptance_criteria: Client queries all 3 APIs. Configurable base URLs via env vars. VCR cassette tests pass deterministically. Handles non-200 responses gracefully.

title: Implement skills-lock.json read/write (Vercel-compatible)
description: WHY: sl skill add/remove/list all need to read and write a lock file tracking installed skills. Must match official Vercel schema for interoperability.

items.item: ReadLocalLock returns empty struct for missing file
ReadLocalLock fails fast on invalid JSON
AddSkill and RemoveSkill helpers work correctly
Round-trip test: write then read produces identical data

design: File: pkg/cli/skills/lock.go. Types: LocalSkillLockFile{Version int, Skills map[string]LocalSkillLockEntry}, LocalSkillLockEntry{Source, Ref, SourceType, ComputedHash string}. Functions: ReadLocalLock(path string) (*LocalSkillLockFile, error) — returns empty on missing file, fails fast on invalid JSON. WriteLocalLock(path string, lock *LocalSkillLockFile) error — sorts skills alphabetically, 2-space indent, trailing newline. AddSkill/RemoveSkill helpers. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/local-lock.ts for exact schema.
acceptance_criteria: Read/write round-trips produce identical JSON. Skills sorted alphabetically. Empty file returns empty struct. Invalid JSON errors with actionable message. Output parseable by npx skills CLI.

title: Implement SHA-256 folder hash computation
description: WHY: skills-lock.json requires a computedHash field matching the Vercel format — SHA-256 of all files in the skill directory, sorted by relative path.

items.item: Sorts files by relative path before hashing
Includes relative path in hash (renames detected)

design: File: pkg/cli/skills/hash.go. Function: ComputeFolderHash(dir string) (string, error). Algorithm: recursively collect all files (skip .git, node_modules), sort by relative path, for each file hash relativePath+contents into a running SHA-256, return hex digest. Must match /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/local-lock.ts:100-115 behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Hash is deterministic across runs. Same content in different order produces same hash. File renames produce different hash. Skips .git and node_modules.

title: US1+US2: Search and Install Skills (P1)
description: WHY: Search and install form the core value loop — users must find skills before installing them. These are the two P1 stories and together deliver the MVP.

items.item: sl skill search displays compact results with footer hint
sl skill search --json returns valid JSON array
sl skill add installs SKILL.md to configured agent paths
sl skill add shows audit before confirmation
sl skill add fires telemetry (respects opt-out)

design: Implements sl skill search (FR-001, FR-002) and sl skill add (FR-004, FR-005, FR-006, FR-012, FR-013, FR-014, FR-015). Requires foundational modules (source parser, client, lock file, hash). Adds discover.go, install.go, telemetry.go, and Cobra commands in skill.go. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/find.ts for search UX, /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/add.ts for install flow.
acceptance_criteria: sl skill search returns results with name/source/installs and footer hint. sl skill add downloads SKILL.md to agent paths, updates lock file, shows audit, fires telemetry.

title: Implement skill discovery (GitHub Trees + git clone)
description: WHY: sl skill add needs to enumerate available skills in a repository before the user can select and install one.

items.item: GitHub Trees API discovery works for public repos
Git clone fallback works for non-GitHub URLs
Auto-retry on GitHub 404 via git clone
Temp directories cleaned up on success and error
VCR cassette test for GitHub Trees API path

design: File: pkg/cli/skills/discover.go. Function: DiscoverSkills(client *Client, source *SkillSource) ([]SkillMetadata, error). Two paths: (1) GitHub fast path (type=github): call GitHub Trees API via client, filter for */SKILL.md paths, fetch each SKILL.md content, parse YAML frontmatter. (2) Git clone fallback (type=git, or GitHub 404): exec git clone --depth 1 to os.MkdirTemp, scan for SKILL.md files using same discovery as /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/skills.ts:108-225, parse frontmatter, cleanup temp dir. If owner/repo shorthand fails GitHub API, auto-retry via git clone https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}.
acceptance_criteria: Discovers skills from GitHub repos via Trees API. Falls back to git clone on 404 or non-GitHub URLs. Parses YAML frontmatter (name, description required). Cleans up temp dirs.

title: Implement skill installation to agent paths
description: WHY: sl skill add needs to write SKILL.md files to the correct agent directories and update the lock file.

items.item: InstallSkill writes SKILL.md to all agent paths
RemoveSkill deletes dirs and lock entry
Agent paths resolved from specledger.yaml + agent registry (NOT constitution)
Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/skills/install.go. Function: InstallSkill(name string, content []byte, agentPaths []string, lockPath string) error. Steps: (1) Read configured agents from specledger.yaml via metadata package (NOT constitution — see #147), (2) look up ConfigDir from internal/agent/registry.go for each agent, (3) create {ConfigDir}/skills/{name}/ directory, (4) write SKILL.md, (5) compute folder hash via hash.ComputeFolderHash(), (6) update lock file via lock.AddSkill(). Also: RemoveSkill(name string, agentPaths []string, lockPath string) error — deletes skill dirs + removes from lock.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md written to each configured agent path. Lock file updated with correct source, ref, sourceType, computedHash. Creates directories if needed. RemoveSkill deletes dirs and lock entry.

title: Implement fire-and-forget telemetry ping
description: WHY: sl skill add should report install events back to skills.sh so installs via SpecLedger are counted in the ecosystem leaderboard.

items.item: Track fires GET request in goroutine
Skipped when DISABLE_TELEMETRY or DO_NOT_TRACK set
Unit tests for gating logic (no network)

design: File: pkg/cli/skills/telemetry.go. Function: Track(event string, params map[string]string). Fires GET https://add-skill.vercel.sh/t?v=specledger-{version}&event={event}&{params} in a goroutine with 3s timeout. Gating: skip if DISABLE_TELEMETRY or DO_NOT_TRACK env var set, skip in CI (check CI, GITHUB_ACTIONS, GITLAB_CI, CIRCLECI, TRAVIS, BUILDKITE, JENKINS_URL, TEAMCITY_VERSION), skip for private repos. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/telemetry.ts:66-81 for CI detection, 131-159 for track function.
acceptance_criteria: Telemetry fires in background, never blocks CLI. Skipped when env vars set. Skipped in CI. Version identified as specledger-{version}.

title: Implement sl skill search command
description: WHY: Users need to discover skills from the skills.sh registry via the CLI.

items.item: Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/skill.go. Register VarSkillCmd (Use: skill) with skillSearchCmd subcommand. Flags: --json, --limit (default 10). runSkillSearch: parse query from args, call client.Search(), display compact table (name, source, installs) with footer hint per docs/design/cli.md, or JSON array with --json. Errors to stderr with suggested fix. Must have --help with 2+ examples. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/find.ts:277-304 for non-interactive output format.
acceptance_criteria: sl skill search query returns matching skills. --json returns valid JSON. --limit controls result count. No results shows friendly message. Network error shows actionable guidance.

title: Implement sl skill add command
description: WHY: Users need to install skills from the registry into their project for their AI agent to use.

items.item: Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/skill.go. skillAddCmd subcommand. Flags: --json, --yes (skip confirmation). runSkillAdd: (1) parse source via ParseSource, (2) discover skills via DiscoverSkills, (3) if multi-skill and no @filter, list and prompt, (4) fetch audit in parallel goroutine (3s timeout), (5) display audit table if available, (6) prompt confirmation unless --yes, (7) install via InstallSkill, (8) fire telemetry. Overwrite check: if skill exists in lock, warn and confirm. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/add.ts:1186-1435 for audit-in-add flow.
acceptance_criteria: Installs SKILL.md to agent paths. Updates lock file. Shows audit before confirm. Fires telemetry. Warns on overwrite. Supports --yes for non-interactive.

title: US3+US4+US5: Info, List, Remove Commands (P2)
description: WHY: After search+install (MVP), users need management capabilities — viewing skill details, listing installed skills, and removing unwanted skills.

items.item: sl skill info shows metadata + 3-partner audit
sl skill list shows installed skills from lock file
sl skill remove deletes skill dirs + lock entry

design: Adds sl skill info (FR-003), sl skill list (FR-008), sl skill remove (FR-007) commands to skill.go. Info reuses client.FetchAudit. List reads from lock file. Remove reuses install.RemoveSkill. All follow Two-Level Output Design (compact + --json).
acceptance_criteria: All 3 commands work independently. Each has compact human output with footer hints and --json for complete output.

title: Implement sl skill info command
description: WHY: Users need to view skill details and security audit results before deciding to install.

items.item: Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/skill.go. skillInfoCmd. Flags: --json. runSkillInfo: parse source, fetch SKILL.md content for metadata (name, description), fetch audit via client.FetchAudit, display both. Human output: skill name/source/description + audit table (Gen, Socket, Snyk columns). JSON output: combined metadata + audit object. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/add.ts:108-154 for audit table format.
acceptance_criteria: Shows skill metadata and all 3 audit partners. Unknown partners show --. High/critical risk shows warning. --json returns combined object.

title: Implement sl skill list command
description: WHY: Users need to see which skills are currently installed in their project.

items.item: Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/skill.go. skillListCmd. Flags: --json. runSkillList: read lock file via lock.ReadLocalLock from project root, display each skill with name and source. Human output: compact list + footer hint. JSON output: array from lock file data. Empty state: helpful message suggesting sl skill search.
acceptance_criteria: Lists all installed skills with name and source. --json returns array. Empty state suggests sl skill search.

title: Implement sl skill remove command
description: WHY: Users need to cleanly uninstall skills they no longer need.

items.item: Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/skill.go. skillRemoveCmd. Flags: --json. runSkillRemove: validate skill name exists in lock file, resolve agent paths, call install.RemoveSkill to delete dirs + update lock. Error if skill not installed (suggest sl skill list). Confirm output.
acceptance_criteria: Removes skill directory from all agent paths. Updates lock file. Errors if not installed with suggestion. --json returns confirmation.

title: US6: Batch Audit Installed Skills (P3)
description: WHY: Team leads need to verify security posture of all installed skills in one command.

items.item: sl skill audit displays 3-partner audit table for all installed skills
sl skill audit skill-name audits single skill
Warning summary if any high/critical risk

design: Adds sl skill audit command to skill.go. Reads installed skills from lock file, queries audit API in batch, displays ATH/Socket/Snyk table for all skills. Warning summary if any high/critical risk. Reference: /Users/vids/specledger/skills/src/add.ts:108-154 for audit table rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Audits all installed skills. Supports single skill audit by name. --json returns per-skill audit data. Warning on high/critical risk.

title: Implement sl skill audit command
description: WHY: Security-conscious teams need batch audit capabilities for all installed skills.

items.item: Network errors show actionable stderr message per CLI design principles (what failed, why, suggested fix)

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/skill.go. skillAuditCmd. Flags: --json, --all (default). Args: optional skill name. runSkillAudit: read lock file, collect all sources/slugs, call client.FetchAudit in batch, display table with Gen/Socket/Snyk columns per skill. If any high/critical → warning at end. Single skill: filter to just that slug. Reference: quickstart.md Scenario 6.
acceptance_criteria: Audits all or single skill. Displays 3-partner table. Warning on critical. --json returns structured data.

title: US7: Embedded sl-skill Agent Skill Template (P2)
description: WHY: AI agents need to know about sl skill commands. Without the embedded skill template, agents cannot discover or use the skill management CLI.

items.item: SKILL.md created following existing skill pattern
Trigger conditions cover find/install/manage skill queries

design: File: pkg/embedded/templates/specledger/skills/sl-skill/SKILL.md. Follow existing pattern from sl-comment, sl-deps, sl-audit skills. Load the skill-creator skill while working on this. Include: trigger conditions (user asks about finding/installing skills), command syntax for all 6 subcommands, JSON output examples, audit interpretation guidance.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md present after sl init. Agent triggers on skill-related queries. Documents all 6 subcommands with examples.

title: Polish: Integration Tests and CLI Compliance
description: WHY: Validate the full CLI end-to-end, verify output formatting, lock file interoperability, and CLI design principle compliance.

items.item: Verified: no Supabase schema changes required (sl skill is API-only, no migrations)

design: File: tests/integration/skills_test.go. Build sl binary, run quickstart scenarios with httptest.Server mock. Record VCR cassettes for unit tests. Verify: compact output with footer hints, valid JSON, stderr errors with suggestions, lock file round-trip, Data CRUD pattern constraints. Reference: /Users/vids/hkjc/tfc-cli/tests/e2e/helpers_test.go for E2E helper pattern.
acceptance_criteria: All quickstart scenarios pass as Go tests. Human output is compact with hints. JSON is valid and complete. Errors follow CLI design principles. Lock file is Vercel-compatible.

title: Create pkg/cli/skills package and add go-vcr dependency
description: WHY: All implementation tasks need the package directory and test dependency in place.
design: Create pkg/cli/skills/ with doc.go. Run go get gopkg.in/dnaeon/go-vcr.v4 (test only). Create tests/testdata/cassettes/skills/ directory. Verify make build succeeds. Reference VCR setup: /Users/vids/hkjc/tfc-cli/internal/tfc/client_test.go.
acceptance_criteria: Package compiles. go-vcr v4 importable in tests. Cassette directory exists. make build passes.

title: Create sl-skill embedded agent skill template
description: WHY: AI agents need an embedded SKILL.md that teaches them when and how to use sl skill commands.

items.item: SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter (name, description)
Trigger conditions for find/install/manage skill queries
All 6 subcommands documented with syntax examples
JSON output examples for agent consumption

design: File: pkg/embedded/templates/specledger/skills/sl-skill/SKILL.md. Follow existing patterns: sl-comment (.claude/skills/sl-comment/), sl-deps, sl-audit. Load the skill-creator skill while working on this. Include: YAML frontmatter (name: sl-skill, description), trigger conditions (user asks about finding/installing/managing agent skills), all 6 subcommands with syntax and examples, JSON output examples for agent parsing, audit interpretation (ATH/Socket/Snyk risk levels).
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md present after sl init. Trigger conditions cover skill-related queries. All 6 subcommands documented with examples.

title: Record VCR cassettes for all API endpoints
description: WHY: Unit tests need deterministic API response recordings to run without network in CI.
design: Files: tests/testdata/cassettes/skills/{search,audit,github_trees,github_raw}.yaml. Use RECORD_CASSETTES=1 pattern from /Users/vids/hkjc/tfc-cli/internal/tfc/record_test.go. Record real responses from skills.sh search API, add-skill.vercel.sh audit API, GitHub Trees API, raw.githubusercontent.com. Create record_test.go in pkg/cli/skills/ with opt-in recording.
acceptance_criteria: 4 cassette files recorded. Tests replay deterministically. Recording is opt-in via env var.

title: Write E2E integration tests for all sl skill commands
description: WHY: Full CLI integration tests validate the complete flow from binary invocation to output format.

items.item: Data CRUD pattern compliance verified for all 6 commands

design: File: tests/integration/skills_test.go. Build sl binary via buildSLBinary() helper. Use httptest.Server as mock API (set SKILLS_API_URL, SKILLS_AUDIT_URL env vars). Test all 8 quickstart scenarios from quickstart.md. Verify: human output compact with footer hints, JSON valid and complete, error messages to stderr with suggestions, lock file round-trip. Reference: /Users/vids/hkjc/tfc-cli/tests/e2e/helpers_test.go for E2E helper pattern.
acceptance_criteria: All quickstart scenarios pass. Human and JSON output verified. Error format verified. Lock file interop verified.
